titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in P59
P59
The New Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John between 2000 and 2010.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. Further information about the procedures followed may be found in the electronic edition of the majuscules at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: lac rest of line tag deleted at 11.52 by Rachel Kevern.
[24] line gap changed to [24] char gap at 21.15 and opening word entity moved at beginning of 2.15 by Rachel Kevern.

Uncertain spaces replaced by rest of verse and rest of line illegible tags at 11.46, spaces and uncertain spaces replaced by rest of verse and rest of page lacunose tags at 12.25, uncertain tags replaced by rest of page and rest of verse tags at 17.26, spaces and uncertain spaces replaced by one line gap tag and editorial note at 18.16, spaces and uncertain spaces replaced by rest of verse gap tag and editorial note at 21.18 by Rachel Kevern.
Gaps and uncertain spaces removed and rest of verse lacuna tag and editorial note added at 2.15 by Rachel Kevern.
Part word element removed and rest of line gap element added to 21.15 by Rachel Kevern.
[8]εις changed to part word gap εις at 1.48 by Rachel Kevern.
[4] changed back to [ημιν] at 11.50 and [12[ changed back to [προεφητευσεν] at 11.51 by Rachel Kevern.
ποισου[8] changed to [α]ποισου[8] at 21.18 by Rachel Kevern.
Part word gap tag added before ζω at 21.7 by Rachel Kevern.
[λογιζεσθα]ι changed back to [λογιζεσθαι] at 11.50 by Rachel Kevern.
[ερουσιν] changed to [αρουσιν] at 11.48, [ημιν] changed to [4] at 11.50 and [προεφητευσεν] changed to [12] at 11.51 by Rachel Kevern.
[λογιζεσθαι] changed to [λογιζεσθα]ι at 11.50 by Rachel Kevern.
9 char gap entity removed and [εδωκας] changed to [6-7] at 17.24 by Rachel Kevern.
Supplied text revised in 11.31 and word break in 21.23 corrected by David Parker.
Removal of ghost page and placement of ab and gap across page boundaries fixed by Catherine Smith.
Adjusted to XML specifications 1.4 (apart from handling of ghost pages) by Hugh Houghton.
Converted from plain text by Catherine Smith using Python conversion scripts 1.0 with manual adjustments.

ab.note: There are traces of ink on this line representing some letters but their identification is not secure.
Fragment ΙΙΙ → contains a line with traces of ink, and traces of a Hermeneia
The editio princeps records letters which cannot now be located at the end of lines 1-4. We record them here.
The editio princeps records letters which cannot now be located at the beginning of lines 1-3. We record them here.

Although we were able to obtain a photograph of the whole fragment, lines 1-5 could not be located for checking. In addition, the editio princeps records letters which cannot now be located in lines 9-10. We record them here.

Although we were able tο obtain a photograph of the whole fragment, lines 1-5 could not be located for checking. Ιn addition, the editio princeps records letters which cannot now be located in lines 5, 8-9 and 12. We record them here.
There are traces of ink on this line representing some letters but their identification is not secure.
There are traces of ink on this line representing some letters but their identification is not secure.
Although we were able tο obtain a photograph, the fragment could not be located for checking.
There are traces of ink on this line representing some letters but their identification is not secure.
We were unable to obtain photographs of this fragment, but the editio princeps has been compared with the original, and we therefοre record it here.
There are traces of ink on this line representing the bottoms of some letters but their identification is not secure.
Fragment VΙΙΙ ↓ contains part of the heading ερμενεια
Hermeneia beneath. Fragment ΙΧ → contains part of a hermeneia
There are traces of ink on this line representing some letters but their identification is not secure.
